sections.title: Grammar: Present Simple for Habits
sections.subtitle: Use Present Simple tense to talk about regular activities

items.usage: Place before the main verb: "I usually wake up at 7 AM."

examples: I usually wake up at 7 AM.
I take my kids to school.
I start my work at 9 AM.
I often have lunch at 12 noon.
I get back to work at 1 PM and finish at 6 PM.
Then, I have dinner with my family.
items.usage: Use time markers and sequence words: then, after, before

items.question: How's your schedule today?
answers: I have a flexible schedule today.
I have a relaxing schedule today.

items.question: Do you have a deadline this week?
answers: Yes, I have a deadline this week.
Yes, I have a deadline on Saturday.
No, I don't have a deadline this week.

items.question: How do you unwind after work today?
answers: I'm going to play games and watch TV after work today.
I'm going out with my family for dinner today.
I'm going to spend time with my kids after work today.

items.question: What do you usually do after lunch?
answers: I usually wake up at 7:00 AM. And I start my day drinking coffee...
I usually work at 1 PM to 6 PM.
But sometimes, I do my workout at home.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: How does your typical workday look in a hybrid setup?

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: On remote days, I start work around 8:30. I check emails and plan my tasks.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Do you prefer working from home or in the office?

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: I like both. At home I'm more productive, but the office is better for meetings.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: What do you usually do on weekends?

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: I try to disconnect from work. I spend time with family or go hiking.

sections.title: Reading: Hybrid Work — A New Daily Routine

paragraphs.text: In recent years, many companies have adopted hybrid working models. Employees divide their time between working from home and going to the office. This change has created new daily routines for workers.

For many people, mornings at home are more relaxed because they do not need to commute. Instead of spending an hour in traffic, they can begin their day earlier and more calmly. Workers often start their day by checking emails, planning tasks, and joining online meetings.
However, hybrid work also requires strong time management skills. Without a manager nearby, employees must organize their own schedules and stay focused on their tasks.
Weekends have also changed for many workers. Because remote work can blur the line between work and personal life, employees must make a conscious effort to disconnect from work and relax.
Overall, hybrid work offers flexibility, but it also requires discipline and good communication.

comprehension.question: What is hybrid work?
comprehension.answer: A working model by dividing the time between working from home and going to the office.

comprehension.question: Why are mornings more relaxed for remote workers?
comprehension.answer: Because they don't need to rush to the office (no commute).

comprehension.question: What skills are important for hybrid workers?
comprehension.answer: Strong time management skills — being organized and staying focused on tasks.

comprehension.question: Why can hybrid work blur work-life boundaries?
comprehension.answer: Some employees don't follow work schedule because there's no manager and no strong time management.

comprehension.question: What advantage does hybrid work provide?
comprehension.answer: It can provide flexible time and save time travelling from home to work.

items.question: What does your daily routine look like?

sections.subtitle: Extra vocabulary and notes from lessons

items.detail: How are you today? / How's your day today? / How's it going? / What's up? → Fine, good, not bad, not much, great
not good, bad, not fine, busy day
cleaning, washing dishes, cooking, doing laundry (washing clothes)
Weekdays/Workdays/School days = Monday to Friday; Weekends = Saturday and Sunday; Holidays = special days with no work (e.g. Lunar New Year, Qingming Festival)
workout = exercise at home / gym / practice; using weights = dumbbells, barbells / heavy equipment for exercise; Leisure Time = past time
-ty = tee (e.g. technology: tek-no-lo-gee); -ly = lee; -gy = gee; discipline = /di-si-plin/
